original_hypothesis.title: EIP-712 _RENTAL_ORDER_TYPEHASH Uses abi.encode Instead of abi.encodePacked for Type String Concatenation
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: In _deriveRentalTypehashes, the rentalOrderTypeHash is computed as keccak256(abi.encode(rentalOrderTypeString, hookTypeString, itemTypeString)) instead of keccak256(abi.encodePacked(...)). Per EIP-712, the struct type hash must be the keccak256 of concatenated type strings. abi.encode adds ABI padding between dynamic bytes arguments, producing a completely different hash from the EIP-712 specification. This makes the on-chain type hash incompatible with any standard EIP-712 implementation.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The rentPayloadTypeHash correctly uses abi.encodePacked in the same function, creating an inconsistency. The wrong type hash means that any off-chain system computing hashes per the EIP-712 spec will derive different values. If the protocol ever needs cross-contract or cross-system hash verification of rental orders, the mismatch becomes a breaking issue.

proof_of_signal.pattern: rentalOrderTypeHash = keccak256(abi.encode(rentalOrderTypeString, hookTypeString, itemTypeString)) uses abi.encode while rentPayloadTypeHash at lines 351-355 correctly uses abi.encodePacked

preconditions: Any operation deriving rental order hashes uses this incorrect type hash

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: abi.encode used for type string concatenation instead of abi.encodePacked
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: abi.encode adds 32-byte length + offset padding per dynamic bytes, changing the hash entirely from EIP-712 spec
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: Internal consistency masks the issue but prevents standard EIP-712 interoperability

analysis_questions: Does any off-chain component depend on standard EIP-712 rental order hash computation?
Could a future integration break when computing the hash per spec?
Could the wrong type hash enable hash collisions that the correct hash would prevent?
validation_requirements: Compare on-chain type hash with spec-compliant keccak256(abi.encodePacked(...)) computation
Verify whether off-chain signer uses same incorrect encoding

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: An off-chain system computes the rental order hash per EIP-712 spec and gets a different result. This breaks signature verification, order matching, or any cross-system hash comparison. If the protocol transitions to off-chain order verification, this mismatch becomes a critical interoperability failure.
prerequisites: Any system computing rental order hashes following EIP-712 spec

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Signature verification failure; potential for hash collisions under non-standard encoding
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All protocol users if off-chain systems rely on standard EIP-712

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test compares on-chain type hash with EIP-712 spec-compliant computation

technical_investigation_focus.execution_timing: N/A — code defect present at deployment
technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Compare hash outputs between on-chain and spec-compliant computation

validation_result.mechanism: EIP-712 type hash encoding error (abi.encode vs abi.encodePacked)
validation_result.impact_summary: The rentalOrderTypeHash is computed with abi.encode instead of abi.encodePacked for type string concatenation, producing a non-standard EIP-712 type hash that differs from what any spec-compliant off-chain implementation would compute.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: In Signer.sol's _deriveRentalTypehashes function, the rentalOrderTypeHash is computed as keccak256(abi.encode(rentalOrderTypeString, hookTypeString, itemTypeString)). Per the EIP-712 specification, the typeHash for a struct that references other structs must be keccak256 of the simple byte concatenation of the type strings (i.e., abi.encodePacked). Using abi.encode on three dynamic bytes arguments produces ABI-standard encoding with: (1) three 32-byte offset pointers, (2) three 32-byte length words, and (3) the padded data for each argument. This fundamentally changes the preimage of the hash. The same function correctly uses abi.encodePacked for rentPayloadTypeHash, confirming this is an inconsistency/bug rather than a design choice.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: Direct code evidence confirms the defect. Signer.sol contains two different encoding approaches for the same purpose (computing EIP-712 composite type hashes) within the same function. rentalOrderTypeHash uses abi.encode (incorrect) while rentPayloadTypeHash uses abi.encodePacked (correct per EIP-712).

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: Uses abi.encode for three dynamic bytes arguments, which adds ABI encoding overhead (offsets, lengths, padding) to the keccak256 preimage. EIP-712 requires simple byte concatenation (abi.encodePacked) of type strings to form the composite type hash. This produces a completely different hash value than the spec requires.
This correctly uses abi.encodePacked for the same type of operation, confirming that the use of abi.encode for rentalOrderTypeHash is inconsistent and erroneous.

calling_sequence: 3. This incorrect hash is stored as immutable _RENTAL_ORDER_TYPEHASH
4. _deriveRentalOrderHash uses _RENTAL_ORDER_TYPEHASH in all rental order hash computations
5. Create.validateOrder -> _rentFromZone -> _deriveRentalOrderHash uses the wrong type hash
6. Stop.stopRent -> _deriveRentalOrderHash uses the same wrong type hash for consistency
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The defective type hash is computed once at deployment in _deriveRentalTypehashes and stored as an immutable. It is then used in every call to _deriveRentalOrderHash (Signer.sol), which is called from Create._rentFromZone (Create.sol) when creating rentals and from Stop.stopRent/stopRentBatch (Stop.sol) when stopping rentals. The internal consistency (same wrong hash used everywhere on-chain) masks the bug operationally but violates the EIP-712 standard.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: No deployment scripts provided that would affect this code-level defect. The bug is in the Signer.sol constructor logic which executes at deployment time regardless of configuration.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: The primary impact is EIP-712 standard non-compliance. Any off-chain system (wallet, dApp frontend, indexer, or cross-protocol integration) that computes the RentalOrder type hash per the EIP-712 specification will derive a different value than what the contract uses. This means: (1) Standard EIP-712 libraries cannot be used to reconstruct rental order hashes off-chain. (2) If a new frontend or integration is built using correct EIP-712 encoding, hash verification will fail. (3) The inconsistency within the same function (encodePacked for rentPayload but encode for rentalOrder) indicates a code quality issue that could lead to harder-to-find integration bugs. While the on-chain system is internally consistent and functions correctly in isolation, the code is objectively wrong per the referenced standard.
validation_result.impact_assessment: MEDIUM-HIGH severity code defect. The on-chain protocol functions correctly due to internal consistency (same wrong hash used for creation and verification). However: (1) It violates the EIP-712 standard that the code explicitly claims to implement. (2) It breaks interoperability with any standard EIP-712 tooling for rental order hashes. (3) The inconsistency with rentPayloadTypeHash (which correctly uses encodePacked) in the same function demonstrates this is a bug, not a design decision. (4) Fixing this requires contract redeployment since the type hash is stored as an immutable.
recommended_mitigations: Change abi.encode to abi.encodePacked in the rentalOrderTypeHash computation: rentalOrderTypeHash = keccak256(abi.encodePacked(rentalOrderTypeString, hookTypeString, itemTypeString))
Add unit tests that compare on-chain type hashes against values computed using standard EIP-712 implementations (e.g., ethers.js TypedDataEncoder)
Audit all other type hash computations for similar abi.encode vs abi.encodePacked inconsistencies
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy the Signer-derived contract (Create or Stop). 2. Read the _RENTAL_ORDER_TYPEHASH immutable. 3. Compute the expected type hash off-chain using standard EIP-712: keccak256(encodePacked('RentalOrder(bytes32 seaportOrderHash,Item[] items,Hook[] hooks,uint8 orderType,address lender,address renter,address rentalWallet,uint256 startTimestamp,uint256 endTimestamp)', 'Hook(address target,uint256 itemIndex,bytes extraData)', 'Item(uint8 itemType,uint8 settleTo,address token,uint256 amount,uint256 identifier)')). 4. Compare the two values - they will differ, confirming the bug.
uncertainties: The exact line numbers cannot be determined with 100% certainty from the provided source since the code doesn't include line numbers, but the code pattern is unambiguously identified in _deriveRentalTypehashes
Whether off-chain components (server-side signer) also use the same incorrect encoding, which would maintain end-to-end consistency despite the standard violation
additional_testing_needed: Compare on-chain _RENTAL_ORDER_TYPEHASH with spec-compliant computation using ethers.js or similar EIP-712 library
Verify whether the off-chain signer (CREATE_SIGNER) uses the same incorrect encoding or standard-compliant encoding
Test that rental order hashes computed off-chain match on-chain values using both encoding methods
